## SAP Stage Alignment

Which SAP stage does this feature primarily serve?

- [ ] Stage 0–2 – Foundation (infrastructure, dependencies, setup)
- [ ] Stage 3–4 – Engine (logic, algorithms, data structures)
- [ ] Stage 5 – Threshold (bifurcation point — architectural decision)
- [ ] Stage 6–7 – Nexus/Lens (integration, I/O, concurrency)
- [ ] Stage 8 – Grounding (stability, monitoring, observability)
- [ ] Stage 9 – Transparency (release, dissolution, renewal)

## Problem Statement

What problem does this feature solve? What SAP stage failure does it prevent or detect?

## Proposed Solution

Describe the solution clearly. Include:
- Which module(s) would be modified
- API changes (if any)
- How this affects the Lyapunov stability guarantees

## Alternatives Considered

What other approaches were considered and why were they rejected?

## Lyapunov Impact

Would this change affect the Numerical Constitution thresholds or stability guarantees?

- [ ] No impact on Lyapunov stability guarantees
- [ ] Improves stability guarantees (explain below)
- [ ] Requires re-evaluation of thresholds (explain below)
